topic: Describe product lifecycle management (PLM) and how Windchill works as a PLM solution
question: Which two business values are primary benefits of implementing Windchill as a PLM solution? (Choose 2)
options: Providing a single source of truth for all enterprise product data
Replacing the company's enterprise resource planning (ERP) system for financial accounting
Managing real-time machine telemetry on the factory floor
Reducing time-to-market by enabling concurrent engineering
Automating payroll and human resources tracking
explanation: The Technical Essentials manual defines Windchill's primary PLM benefits as providing a single source of truth for product data and enabling concurrent engineering to reduce time-to-market. ERP and HR functions are outside its core scope.

topic: Describe product lifecycle management (PLM) and how Windchill works as a PLM solution
question: Which two enterprise systems are most commonly integrated with Windchill to establish a complete digital thread? (Choose 2)
options: Customer Relationship Management (CRM)
Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP)
Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES)
Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS)
IT Service Management (ITSM)
explanation: The manual states that integrating Windchill with ERP and MES systems allows organizations to seamlessly pass engineering BOMs to manufacturing, establishing a digital thread.

topic: Describe product lifecycle management (PLM) and how Windchill works as a PLM solution
question: What are two key capabilities of the Windchill part-centric product development approach? (Choose 2)
options: The CAD document is the primary object that drives the Bill of Materials
The WTPart acts as the central container for all related product information
WTParts can structurally link to multiple CAD documents, drawings, and specifications
It requires users to manually type BOM structures into the ERP system
It restricts parts from having downstream manufacturing equivalents
explanation: The part-centric approach uses the WTPart to represent the physical component, acting as a central hub that links to CAD models, drawings, and documents, allowing a comprehensive view of the product.

topic: Describe product lifecycle management (PLM) and how Windchill works as a PLM solution
question: Which three statements accurately describe the Windchill client environment? (Choose 3)
options: It requires a heavy Java applet installation on all client machines
It is primarily accessed via a standard web browser
It offers an embedded browser within Creo Parametric
It supports interactions via the Microsoft Office suite using DTI
It requires direct SQL access to the Oracle database for general users
explanation: The Advanced Configuration manual identifies web browsers, the Creo embedded browser, and Microsoft Office (via Desktop Integration) as the primary client interfaces. Direct SQL access is never permitted for end users.

topic: Describe product lifecycle management (PLM) and how Windchill works as a PLM solution
question: When defining closed-loop quality in Windchill, which two modules are typically utilized to capture and correct field issues? (Choose 2)
options: Windchill Nonconformance (NC)
Windchill ProjectLink
Windchill Customer Experience Management (CEM)
Windchill PartsLink
Windchill Requirements management
explanation: The Technical Essentials manual's quality management section highlights Nonconformance (NC) and Customer Experience Management (CEM) as the core tools for capturing field issues and feeding them back into the engineering change process.

topic: Describe product lifecycle management (PLM) and how Windchill works as a PLM solution
question: Which two statements reflect PTC's recommended best practices for managing legacy data during a Windchill PLM implementation? (Choose 2)
options: Migrate all legacy data regardless of its age or relevance
Cleanse and validate legacy data before migration
Use the Windchill Bulk Migrator (WBM) for large-scale data imports
Manually recreate all legacy CAD models in Creo before importing
Import legacy data directly into the Site context for global access
explanation: Best practices dictate cleansing data prior to migration and utilizing the Windchill Bulk Migrator (WBM) for efficiency. Recreating models manually is inefficient, and data should go to Products/Libraries, not the Site context.

topic: Describe the PLM foundational object model and relationships (parts, CAD, documents, etc.)
question: Which two object types inherit directly from the WTObject class in Windchill? (Choose 2)
options: wt.doc.WTDocument
wt.fc.PersistInfo
wt.part.WTPart
java.lang.String
wt.util.WTProperties
explanation: WTDocument and WTPart are both business objects that inherit from WTObject, which represents all persistable data types in the Windchill foundation.

topic: Describe the PLM foundational object model and relationships (parts, CAD, documents, etc.)
question: What are two distinct characteristics of an EPMDocument in Windchill? (Choose 2)
options: It represents a CAD file generated by an authoring application
It can only be created by an administrator in the Site context
It manages the dependencies and references between CAD models
It is used exclusively for Microsoft Word documents
It cannot be associated with a WTPart
explanation: EPMDocuments represent CAD files and are designed specifically to understand and manage complex CAD-to-CAD reference dependencies.

topic: Describe the PLM foundational object model and relationships (parts, CAD, documents, etc.)
question: Which three link types are commonly used to associate documents to a WTPart? (Choose 3)
options: Described By
Calculated Reference
Reference
Build Rule
Primary Content
explanation: A WTPart can be associated with documents via 'Described By' or 'Reference' links, and associated with EPMDocuments (CAD) via a 'Build Rule' (Owner/Image link).

topic: Describe the PLM foundational object model and relationships (parts, CAD, documents, etc.)
question: When configuring an Owner association between an EPMDocument and a WTPart, which two behaviors occur? (Choose 2)
options: The CAD document structure drives the WTPart BOM structure
The WTPart is deleted if the CAD document is checked out
The WTPart automatically adopts the CAD document's life cycle state
Attributes can be mapped and synchronized between the two objects
The EPMDocument becomes read-only permanently
explanation: An Owner link allows the CAD structure to build the WTPart BOM (Build Rule) and facilitates bi-directional attribute synchronization between the CAD parameter and Windchill attribute.

topic: Describe the PLM foundational object model and relationships (parts, CAD, documents, etc.)
question: Which two statements accurately describe the difference between WTDocument and WTDocumentMaster? (Choose 2)
options: WTDocumentMaster holds iteration-specific data like the life cycle state
WTDocumentMaster holds attributes that remain constant across all versions, such as the Number
WTDocument represents a specific iteration of the document
WTDocument is used for CAD files while WTDocumentMaster is used for text files
Only the WTDocumentMaster can be checked out
explanation: The Master object holds constant data (Name, Number) across all versions, while the WTDocument object holds version/iteration-specific data (State, Version ID, Content).

topic: Describe the PLM foundational object model and relationships (parts, CAD, documents, etc.)
question: When dealing with Windchill content management, what are two valid ways primary content differs from secondary content? (Choose 2)
options: An object can have only one primary content file
Primary content cannot be downloaded by a user
Secondary content is typically used for attachments like PDFs or reference images
Secondary content dictates the object's version scheme
Primary content is stored in the database, secondary content in the vault
explanation: Windchill objects (like WTDocument) can have exactly one primary content file representing the main file, and multiple secondary content files representing attachments or renditions.

topic: Describe the PLM foundational object model and relationships (parts, CAD, documents, etc.)
question: Which three association types exist between an EPMDocument and a WTPart? (Choose 3)
options: Owner
Image
Contributing Image
Calculated
Derived
explanation: The PTC standard associations between EPMDocuments and WTParts are Owner (driving), Image (non-driving), and Contributing Image (partially driving). Calculated and Derived are not standard association link types.

topic: Identify Windchill capabilities (the use of contexts, libraries, etc.)
question: Which two context types are designed specifically to hold design data and CAD models? (Choose 2)
options: Site Context
Product Context
Organization Context
Project Context
Program Context
explanation: Products and Projects are standard application contexts designed to store operational design data, whereas Site, Organization, and Program contexts are largely for administrative data or grouping.

topic: Identify Windchill capabilities (the use of contexts, libraries, etc.)
question: What are two defining characteristics of a Windchill Library context? (Choose 2)
options: It is used to store standard parts and enterprise-wide reference documents
Objects inside it cannot be checked out or revised
It is typically managed by a librarian or a small group of highly trusted administrators
It automatically deletes objects that haven't been accessed in 90 days
It sits above the Site context in the hierarchy
explanation: Libraries are used for shared, standardized components (like fasteners) and company policies, and are tightly controlled by a librarian role to prevent unauthorized changes to enterprise standards.

topic: Identify Windchill capabilities (the use of contexts, libraries, etc.)
question: Which two features are unique to a Project context compared to a Product context? (Choose 2)
options: The ability to create folders
The ability to assign tasks, milestones, and project plans
The ability to invite external suppliers as Guests without exposing the main Product data
The ability to store WTParts
The ability to define object initialization rules
explanation: Projects include project management features (plans, tasks, timelines) and are often used as secure, isolated environments to collaborate with external vendors before sending data back to a Product.

topic: Identify Windchill capabilities (the use of contexts, libraries, etc.)
question: When moving an object from a Project context to a Product context (Send to PDM), which two actions occur? (Choose 2)
options: The object adopts the access control policies of the destination Product
The object's version history is permanently deleted
The object becomes shared and is visible in both contexts
The system bypasses all OIRs in the destination context
The object's life cycle may be reassigned based on the destination's rules
explanation: Sending to PDM changes the object's primary container, subjecting it to the domain policies, access control, and OIR-driven life cycles of the new Product context.

topic: Identify Windchill capabilities (the use of contexts, libraries, etc.)
question: Which three statements regarding context teams and shared teams are true? (Choose 3)
options: A context can utilize both a local context team and a shared team simultaneously
Shared teams are defined at the Organization level
Context managers cannot add local users if a shared team is assigned
Modifying a shared team updates the membership for all contexts using it
Shared teams are created in the Site context only
explanation: Shared teams reside at the Organization level and propagate updates to all utilizing contexts. A context can have a local team alongside a shared team to accommodate specific local users.

topic: Identify Windchill capabilities (the use of contexts, libraries, etc.)
question: Which two Windchill features are configured primarily at the Organization context rather than the Product context? (Choose 2)
options: Shared Teams
Project Plans
Enterprise LDAP directory mapping
Global Reusable Attributes
Product-specific folder structures
explanation: Shared Teams and LDAP integration (Restricted Directory Search) are managed at the Organization level, while project plans and specific folder structures belong to lower-level application contexts.

topic: Create and manage business objects (object initialization rules [OIRs], etc.)
question: Which two behaviors can be automated when an object is created using an Object Initialization Rule (OIR)? (Choose 2)
options: Assigning the default life cycle template
Checking the object into the vault immediately
Assigning the default folder location
Sending an email to the CEO
Changing the database schema
explanation: OIRs automate instantiation behaviors, specifically setting the default folder path, numbering sequence, life cycle template, and team template.

topic: Create and manage business objects (object initialization rules [OIRs], etc.)
question: When defining an OIR for auto-numbering, which three XML elements are typically utilized? (Choose 3)
options: AttrValue
NumberingAttributeAlgorithm
Arg
LifeCycleTemplateAlgorithm
SQLQuery
explanation: The OIR uses the <AttrValue> tag for the 'number' attribute, specifies the <Algorithm class="...NumberingAttributeAlgorithm">, and uses <Arg> to pass the sequence name.

topic: Create and manage business objects (object initialization rules [OIRs], etc.)
question: If an OIR is modified at the Organization level, which two statements are true regarding existing and new objects? (Choose 2)
options: Existing objects are automatically moved to the new folder specified in the OIR
New objects created after the change will follow the new rule
Existing objects' life cycles are updated to match the new OIR
The change affects all products in the organization unless overridden by a product-level OIR
The Site-level OIR will permanently override the Organization OIR
explanation: OIRs only fire upon object instantiation (creation). They do not retroactively modify existing objects. An Organization OIR cascades down to Products unless explicitly overridden.

topic: Create and manage business objects (object initialization rules [OIRs], etc.)
question: Which two XML algorithms can be used within an OIR to set the versioning scheme of a document? (Choose 2)
options: VersionSchemeAttributeAlgorithm
StateBasedVersioningAlgorithm
LifeCycleTemplateAttributeAlgorithm
ObjectRevisionAlgorithm
RuleBasedVersioningAlgorithm
explanation: Versioning is assigned either directly via the VersionSchemeAttributeAlgorithm or indirectly by assigning a life cycle (LifeCycleTemplateAttributeAlgorithm) that uses state-based versioning.

topic: Create and manage business objects (object initialization rules [OIRs], etc.)
question: When configuring constraints in the Type and Attribute Manager, which two constraints prevent a user from leaving a field blank? (Choose 2)
options: Legal Value Set
Required
Suggested Value Set
String Length (with a minimum > 0)
Regular Expression (allowing empty strings)
explanation: The 'Required' constraint explicitly mandates a value. A 'String Length' constraint with a minimum length greater than zero implicitly prevents the field from being blank.

topic: Create and manage business objects (object initialization rules [OIRs], etc.)
question: Which three actions are permitted on an alias attribute in Windchill? (Choose 3)
options: Displaying it in a table view
Using it as search criteria in Advanced Search
Viewing it on the object's information page
Editing its value directly from the alias attribute's parent object
Using it to store secondary content
explanation: Alias attributes are read-only views of data stored on a related object. They can be searched, viewed, and added to tables, but they cannot be edited directly from the referencing object.

topic: Create and manage business objects (object initialization rules [OIRs], etc.)
question: What two conditions must be met for a Cascading Attribute rule to function properly? (Choose 2)
options: The driven attribute must be constrained by a legal value list
The rule must not create a circular dependency
The attributes must be defined exclusively at the Site level
An IF/THEN condition must be mapped logically between the driver and driven attributes
The attributes must be local, not global
explanation: Cascading attributes require a logical IF/THEN driving relationship and will fail to save if the system detects a circular dependency between the attributes.

topic: Manage object change and release (life cycles, workflows, change notices, etc.)
question: Which two objects are considered part of the formal Change Implementation and Review stages? (Choose 2)
options: Problem Report
Change Notice
Change Task
Promotion Request
Variance
explanation: The Change Notice is the formal authorization for implementation, and it contains Change Tasks which detail the specific work to be done and reviewed.

topic: Manage object change and release (life cycles, workflows, change notices, etc.)
question: In an advanced life cycle, which two features differentiate it from a basic life cycle? (Choose 2)
options: The ability to associate workflow templates with states
The ability to define state-specific access control grants
The ability to have more than five states
The ability to rename states
The ability to hide the life cycle from OIRs
explanation: Advanced life cycles allow administrators to trigger workflows upon entering a phase and grant state-specific role access control overrides.

topic: Manage object change and release (life cycles, workflows, change notices, etc.)
question: When a Change Notice is approved, what happens to the affected objects listed in its Change Tasks? (Choose 2)
options: They are deleted from the vault
They automatically transition through the 'Change' life cycle path (e.g., to Released)
Their iterations are locked and a new revision is created if specified
They are exported to the ERP system immediately
They are sent to a Project context
explanation: An approved Change Notice executes the change intent, locking the current iteration and moving the affected objects through the 'Change' transition path to their target release state.

topic: Manage object change and release (life cycles, workflows, change notices, etc.)
question: Which three statements are true regarding Windchill Workflow Expression Robots? (Choose 3)
options: They execute Java code directly within the workflow process
They run in the background method server if configured to do so
They pause the workflow until a user completes a manual task
They can be used to programmatically set the state of an object
They require the user to input a password to execute
explanation: Expression robots are automated nodes that execute Java API code (like setting a state) in the background server queue, requiring no manual user interaction.

topic: Manage object change and release (life cycles, workflows, change notices, etc.)
question: When configuring a workflow task, what two routing options are available in a 'Conditional' routing node? (Choose 2)
options: Routing based on a user's manual selection from a list of options
Routing based on the evaluation of a Java boolean expression
Routing based on the phase of the moon
Routing based on the outcome of a custom Java code block comparing object attributes
Routing based strictly on alphabetical order of the task name
explanation: Conditional nodes automatically route the workflow path by evaluating a Java boolean expression or a block of custom Java code that returns a specific routing string.

topic: Manage object change and release (life cycles, workflows, change notices, etc.)
question: Which two transition types are strictly associated with the formal Change Management process? (Choose 2)
options: Revise
Change
Promote
Production Released
Set State
explanation: The 'Change', 'Production Released', and 'Obsolescence' transitions define paths executed specifically by the approval of a Change Notice.

topic: Manage object change and release (life cycles, workflows, change notices, etc.)
question: If a promotion request is rejected by an approver, which two outcomes typically occur based on the OOTB promotion workflow? (Choose 2)
options: The objects remain in their current 'In Work' state
The objects are automatically deleted
The promotion request object itself is routed back to the creator for rework or cancellation
The objects are marked as Obsolete
The system generates a Problem Report
explanation: When rejected, the Promotion Request workflow routes a rework task back to the creator, and the target objects remain unchanged in their pre-promotion state.

topic: Describe setting up a Windchill system (installation, architecture, etc.)
question: During a Windchill installation, which two core components must be configured to establish the Database Tier? (Choose 2)
options: Apache Web Server
Oracle or SQL Server Database
Windchill Directory Server (LDAP)
Creo Parametric
Windchill Method Server
explanation: The database tier consists of the relational database (Oracle/SQL Server) for PLM metadata and the administrative directory server (LDAP) for user authentication.

topic: Describe setting up a Windchill system (installation, architecture, etc.)
question: Which three statements describe the function of the Windchill Method Server? (Choose 3)
options: It contains the embedded Tomcat servlet engine
It processes client transactions and business logic
It interacts directly with the database to store and retrieve metadata
It acts as a load balancer for external web traffic
It renders the 3D viewables for Creo View
explanation: The Method Server is the core Java application tier component that contains Tomcat, processes business logic, and executes database transactions.

topic: Describe setting up a Windchill system (installation, architecture, etc.)
question: When scaling a Windchill environment, what are two benefits of configuring a clustered architecture? (Choose 2)
options: Eliminating the need for a relational database
Reducing the total amount of disk space needed for vaulting
High availability and failover protection if one server goes down
Bypassing the need for LDAP authentication
Load balancing of user requests across multiple method servers
explanation: Clustering provides high availability (failover) and horizontal scalability by balancing the user load across multiple nodes.

topic: Describe setting up a Windchill system (installation, architecture, etc.)
question: Which two configuration files are managed using the `xconfmanager` utility? (Choose 2)
options: index.html
wt.properties
web.xml
db.properties
OIR.xml
explanation: The `xconfmanager` utility manages and propagates property changes from `site.xconf` down to target property files like `wt.properties` and `db.properties`.

topic: Describe setting up a Windchill system (installation, architecture, etc.)
question: What two mechanisms are used to tune Windchill server memory and cache sizes? (Choose 2)
options: Windchill Configuration Assistant (WCA)
Restarting the Apache web server
Editing the OIR.xml file
Deleting the local workspace cache
Modifying JVM arguments in the Server Manager configuration
explanation: WCA calculates the optimal cache and memory settings, while JVM arguments dictate the heap size allocated to the Method Server and Server Manager.

topic: Describe setting up a Windchill system (installation, architecture, etc.)
question: Which two services must be started in the correct order for Windchill to initialize properly? (Choose 2)
options: The Apache Web Server must start before the Database
Creo Parametric must be running before the Method Server starts
The Database service (Oracle/SQL) must start before Windchill
The Windchill Directory Server must start before Windchill
The CAD worker must start before the Database
explanation: Windchill requires both the database and the LDAP directory to be running and accessible before the Method Server can initialize.

topic: Perform basic administration tasks to the Windchill system (services, properties, utilities, etc.)
question: Which two tasks are considered routine Windchill administrative maintenance? (Choose 2)
options: Rewriting the core Windchill Java source code
Monitoring background queues for failed entries
Manually editing the site.xconf file using Microsoft Word
Reinstalling the Oracle database weekly
Reviewing the Server Status page and Method Server logs
explanation: Routine maintenance involves monitoring system health via the Server Status page, checking logs, and ensuring background queues are processing without failures.

topic: Perform basic administration tasks to the Windchill system (services, properties, utilities, etc.)
question: When managing background queues in Windchill, what two actions can an administrator take on a 'Severe' failed queue entry? (Choose 2)
options: Send the entry to the CAD worker
Convert the entry into a Change Notice
Promote the entry to a foreground process
Delete the entry
Reset and re-execute the entry after resolving the underlying issue
explanation: Queue Manager allows administrators to view failed entries, analyze the stack trace, and either delete the entry or reset it to execute again once the issue is fixed.

topic: Perform basic administration tasks to the Windchill system (services, properties, utilities, etc.)
question: Which two statements about the Windchill vaulting process are correct? (Choose 2)
options: Vaulting offloads heavy content files (like CAD) from the database to the file system
Encrypts passwords in the LDAP server
Vaulting rules determine which folders specific files are saved to based on life cycle state or context
Vaulting replaces the need for a database backup
Vaulting compresses all files into a single ZIP archive
explanation: File vaults store the physical BLOB data (CAD, PDFs) on the file system to keep the database lightweight. Vaulting rules dictate routing logic (e.g., routing Released files to a specific read-only mount).

topic: Perform basic administration tasks to the Windchill system (services, properties, utilities, etc.)
question: Which three actions are strictly performed using the `windchill shell` command line? (Choose 3)
options: Creating a new user account
Starting or stopping the Windchill server using `windchill start` or `windchill stop`
Approving a Change Notice
Running the `xconfmanager` utility
Executing the `WContentVerify` tool
explanation: The Windchill shell environment sets the necessary Java path variables to run administrative command-line tools like xconfmanager, start/stop scripts, and WContentVerify.

topic: Perform basic administration tasks to the Windchill system (services, properties, utilities, etc.)
question: To perform a 'hot backup' of a Windchill environment, what two steps must be taken to ensure data consistency? (Choose 2)
options: Stop the Method Server entirely
Uninstall the Apache web server
Suspend all background queues
Place the database in backup mode
Delete the local cache
explanation: A hot backup allows users to remain logged in. To ensure consistency without shutting down, queues must be suspended and the database placed into hot-backup mode.

topic: Perform basic administration tasks to the Windchill system (services, properties, utilities, etc.)
question: What two purposes does the `WinRU` (Windchill Rehost Utility) serve? (Choose 2)
options: Changing the host name or directory structure of an existing Windchill system
Upgrading Windchill from version 11 to 12
Migrating CAD data from SolidWorks to Creo
Managing workflow state transitions
Cloning a production environment to create a test environment
explanation: The Rehost Utility is used to clone systems for testing/development or to safely change the hostname and LDAP structure of a deployed system.

topic: Use Windchill Navigate in conjunction with Windchill
question: What are two primary benefits of using Windchill Navigate? (Choose 2)
options: It replaces Creo Parametric for heavy CAD modeling
It provides a simplified, app-based UI for non-expert users to view PLM data
It serves as the backend database for Windchill
It combines data from Windchill with external enterprise systems like ERP using ThingWorx
It allows administrators to write object initialization rules visually
explanation: Navigate is built on ThingWorx to provide lightweight, role-based apps for downstream users to view drawings, parts lists, and mashup data from PLM and ERP.

topic: Use Windchill Navigate in conjunction with Windchill
question: Which two OOTB Navigate apps are typically used by a manufacturing floor worker? (Choose 2)
options: System Configuration Collector
Policy Administrator
View Document
Type and Attribute Manager
View Drawing
explanation: View Document and View Drawing are standard Navigate apps designed for non-engineers to quickly pull up PDF specs or CAD drawings without learning the full Windchill UI.

topic: Use Windchill Navigate in conjunction with Windchill
question: Which two technologies form the foundation of Windchill Navigate? (Choose 2)
options: ThingWorx Foundation
Microsoft Access
Apache Struts
Windchill REST Services
PHP
explanation: Navigate is an IoT mashup built on the ThingWorx platform, which pulls real-time PLM data by communicating with the Windchill REST Services API.

topic: Use Windchill Navigate in conjunction with Windchill
question: When tailoring Windchill Navigate, which two actions are supported via the Navigate Administrator console? (Choose 2)
options: Modifying the Oracle database schema
Adding custom Windchill attributes to the View Part app
Hiding specific attributes from being displayed in an app
Rewriting the core Java method server
Changing the CAD worker publishing interval
explanation: The Navigate admin UI allows tailoring apps by selecting which OOTB or custom Windchill attributes are visible to the end user, without requiring ThingWorx development.

topic: Use Windchill Navigate in conjunction with Windchill
question: If a user requires access to a Windchill Navigate app, which two security requirements must be met? (Choose 2)
options: The user must be assigned the Site Administrator role
The user must have a Creo Parametric license
The user must log in from a mobile device
The user must have an account in the Windchill directory server (LDAP)
The user must have appropriate Read access control permissions in Windchill to view the underlying data
explanation: Navigate enforces Windchill's security model. The user must be authenticated, and they will only see data in Navigate that they have Windchill access control permissions to view.
